message:
chore(beads): close br-r37-c1-y8m6q (file-like in readwrite)

Fixed in commit ca3ecc5. write_/read_ multiline_adjlist and
weighted_edgelist now accept BytesIO/StringIO matching nx's
@open_file semantics. Cross-compat with nx-written buffers
verified.

Sweep: 6607 passed, 0 failed (+7 new regression tests).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -143,7 +143,7 @@
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-xiqgr","title":"spectral_ordering returns nodes in reversed/different order from nx","description":"fnx.spectral_ordering returns nodes in a different order than nx — mathematically equivalent (the Fiedler eigenvector's sign is arbitrary, so both [a,b,c,d,e] and [e,d,c,b,a] are valid spectral orderings) but the drop-in contract requires matching nx's choice exactly.

Repro:
  edges = [(0,1),(1,2),(2,3),(3,4)] (path)
  fnx -> [4, 3, 2, 1, 0]
  nx  -> [0, 1, 2, 3, 4]
  same with method='lanczos' produces different tiebreak orderings even on graphs where the default method matches.

Drop-in code that compared spectral_ordering(G) to a reference list (often used as an input to nested-dissection-style ordering or graph layout) silently broke.

Fix: delegate spectral_ordering to nx via _call_networkx_for_parity (mirrors the existing pattern for other delegated algorithms).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T15:03:30.929863213Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T15:06:32.044081576Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T15:06:32.042029210Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-xsrkr","title":"parity: Graph instances are content-equal and unhashable, nx uses identity-equal and hashable","description":"fnx.Graph defines content-based __eq__ in the Rust binding (path_graph(3) == path_graph(3) returns True) and is unhashable (Python sets __hash__ = None when __eq__ is defined). nx uses object's default identity-based __eq__ and __hash__: nx.path_graph(3) == nx.path_graph(3) returns False, nx.Graph() is hashable for use in sets/dict keys. Drop-in code that puts graphs in sets/dicts (set([G1, G2])) raises TypeError on fnx; code that compares graph identity (if G is reference_graph or G == reference_graph) gets opposite answers on fnx vs nx. Fix: override __eq__ and __hash__ on all four Graph classes to use object's defaults (identity-based) matching nx exactly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T07:42:25.251403517Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T07:50:30.218447403Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T07:50:30.216475861Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 64e1627. Graph/DiGraph/MultiGraph/MultiDiGraph now use object's identity-based __eq__ and __hash__ matching nx exactly. set/dict containing graphs now works on fnx. 29 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-xy4xf","title":"parity: branching/arborescence functions coerce int weights to float","description":"fnx.maximum_branching, fnx.minimum_branching, fnx.maximum_spanning_arborescence, fnx.minimum_spanning_arborescence on directed graphs return edge dicts with weight values coerced to float. nx preserves the original weight type (int weights stay int). Drop-in code that asserts isinstance(d['weight'], int) breaks. Default weight (1, when no edge has the weight attr) is also affected — fnx returns 1.0, nx returns 1. Root: Rust binding coerces to f64. Fix: after the Rust call, restore each retained edge's attribute dict from the source graph so the caller sees the original int (or whatever) type.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T10:01:54.571417216Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T10:06:01.896383879Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T10:06:01.894373329Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit bb2acaa. Branching/arborescence wrappers now preserve weight types from source matching nx. 9 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"br-r37-c1-y8m6q","title":"write_weighted_edgelist and write_multiline_adjlist reject file-like objects","description":"fnx.write_weighted_edgelist(G, BytesIO()) and fnx.write_multiline_adjlist(G, BytesIO()) raise TypeError('expected str, bytes or os.PathLike object, not BytesIO'). nx accepts file-like objects (BytesIO) for these functions via the @open_file decorator and writes the encoded bytes directly.

Repro:
  >>> import io
  >>> g = fnx.Graph([(1, 2, {'weight': 1.5})])
  >>> nx.write_weighted_edgelist(g, io.BytesIO())  # OK, writes b'1 2 1.5\n'
  >>> fnx.write_weighted_edgelist(g, io.BytesIO())  # TypeError

Root cause: python/franken_networkx/readwrite/__init__.py:1099 (write_weighted_edgelist) and :998 (write_multiline_adjlist) blindly call open(path, 'w', ...) which only accepts file paths. write_edgelist (line 287, _write_bytes) and write_pajek (line 746) already implement the dual-path handling. Apply the same pattern.

Drop-in code that streams to BytesIO (very common in tests, network handlers, in-memory pipelines) silently breaks.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T03:49:03.645856336Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T03:49:32.592338034Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-r37-c1-y8m6q","title":"write_weighted_edgelist and write_multiline_adjlist reject file-like objects","description":"fnx.write_weighted_edgelist(G, BytesIO()) and fnx.write_multiline_adjlist(G, BytesIO()) raise TypeError('expected str, bytes or os.PathLike object, not BytesIO'). nx accepts file-like objects (BytesIO) for these functions via the @open_file decorator and writes the encoded bytes directly.

Repro:
  >>> import io
  >>> g = fnx.Graph([(1, 2, {'weight': 1.5})])
  >>> nx.write_weighted_edgelist(g, io.BytesIO())  # OK, writes b'1 2 1.5\n'
  >>> fnx.write_weighted_edgelist(g, io.BytesIO())  # TypeError

Root cause: python/franken_networkx/readwrite/__init__.py:1099 (write_weighted_edgelist) and :998 (write_multiline_adjlist) blindly call open(path, 'w', ...) which only accepts file paths. write_edgelist (line 287, _write_bytes) and write_pajek (line 746) already implement the dual-path handling. Apply the same pattern.

Drop-in code that streams to BytesIO (very common in tests, network handlers, in-memory pipelines) silently breaks.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T03:49:03.645856336Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T03:55:37.775071124Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T03:55:37.772318030Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-yf4tf","title":"parity: NetworkXNotImplemented messages diverge from nx's standard 'not implemented for <type> type' format","description":"Three functions raise NetworkXNotImplemented with custom messages instead of nx's standard format: (1) immediate_dominators raises 'immediate_dominators is not defined for undirected graphs.'; nx raises 'not implemented for undirected type'. (2) is_strongly_connected raises 'is_strongly_connected is not defined for undirected graphs. Use is_connected instead.'; nx raises 'not implemented for undirected type'. (3) is_weakly_connected raises similar custom message vs nx's standard. Drop-in code that asserts on the exact NetworkXNotImplemented message text breaks. Fix: align messages to nx's @not_implemented_for decorator format ('not implemented for <type> type').","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T09:23:41.000065082Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T09:26:14.013568279Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T09:26:14.011015876Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 7879d79. is_strongly_connected, is_weakly_connected, and immediate_dominators now raise NetworkXNotImplemented with nx's standard 'not implemented for <type> type' message. 7 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-ygr0e","title":"parity: node_classification harmonic_function + local_and_global_consistency wrappers (2 functions)","description":"networkx.algorithms.node_classification exposes two label-propagation classifiers that franken_networkx does not surface: harmonic_function(G, max_iter=30, label_name=label) and local_and_global_consistency(G, alpha=0.99, max_iter=30, label_name=label). Both are graph-based semi-supervised learning algorithms. Add Python wrappers in __init__.py via _call_networkx_submodule_for_parity(algorithms.node_classification, ...), export at top-level and add to __all__.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-25T20:49:44.313573017Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T21:06:42.170412280Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T21:06:42.168295571Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 3106ac9: harmonic_function + local_and_global_consistency wrappers; both verified on labeled chain to return identical classifications to nx.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-yrj7i","title":"parity: generate_random_paths defaulted weight=None and ignored edge weights","description":"fnx.generate_random_paths(G, sample_size) defaulted weight=None vs nx weight='weight'. Worse, the implementation always did a uniform random.choice over neighbors regardless of the weight kwarg — even if weight='weight' was passed, edge weights were ignored. Concrete reproducer: G with edges (0,1,w=10) and (0,2,w=1); fnx.generate_random_paths(G, 500, path_length=1, source=0, seed=42, weight='weight') visited nodes 1 and 2 roughly equally; nx visits 1 ~10x more often. Fixed both: aligned default to 'weight' and rewrote the walk to call random.choices(nbrs, weights=...) when weight is set, falling back to uniform when weight=None. MultiGraph aggregates parallel-edge weights.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T03:35:12.416280200Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T03:35:58.963972417Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T03:35:58.962095164Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 6f21573. weight='weight' now matches nx default; implementation honours weights via random.choices. 9 parity tests in test_generate_random_paths_parity.py freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
